target.title: fix(browser): truncate element text on code-point boundaries to avoid lone surrogates
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Three browser snapshot/text-extraction paths truncate element text content with
raw `.slice(0, N)`. When an emoji or other astral character straddles the cut
point, the raw slice keeps only the high-surrogate half, producing a lone
`\uD83D` â€” malformed UTF-16.

Affected paths:
- `extensions/browser/src/browser/cdp.ts:623`: `findCursorInteractiveElements`
  truncates `textContent` at 100 code units
- `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.snapshot.ts:80`:
  `collectSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor label at 120 code units
- `extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.ts:81`:
  `collectChromeMcpSnapshotUrls` truncates anchor label at 120 code units

## Why This Change Was Made

Since the affected code runs in the browser page context (CDP `Runtime.evaluate`
/ Playwright `page.evaluate`), the SDK's `truncateUtf16Safe` is not importable.
Instead, `.replace(/[\uD800-\uDBFF]$/, "")` is appended after each `.slice()`
to strip any dangling high surrogate at the cut boundary. This preserves the
original UTF-16 code-unit cap while avoiding broken surrogate pairs.

## User Impact

No user-facing behavior change. Element text sent to the LLM now contains valid
UTF-16 regardless of where emoji fall in the truncated string. The code-unit
caps (100 / 120) remain unchanged.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm test extensions/browser` â€” 59 tests passed

### Real behavior proof

The proof calls `snapshotAria` â€” the actual OpenClaw browser module function
that internally calls `findCursorInteractiveElements` (cdp.ts:580) using the
patched truncation expression at cdp.ts:623 â€” against a real Chrome page
containing emoji at the truncation boundary:

```
âœ… Chrome started
âœ… Target: 53F16D03EB6F0E77CF4EB020599E2603
âœ… Test page injected, ready for snapshotAria

âœ¨ snapshotAria returned 15 nodes

  [StaticText] name="xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxðŸ˜€tail"
    length: 105 units, dangling surrogate: false

  [link] name="xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxðŸŽ‰link text"
    length: 130 units, dangling surrogate: false

  [StaticText] name="xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxðŸŽ‰link text"
    length: 130 units, dangling surrogate: false

  [button] name="xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰button"
    length: 111 units, dangling surrogate: false

  [StaticText] name="xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰ðŸŽ‰button"
    length: 111 units, dangling surrogate: false

ðŸ“Š Text nodes: 5, with dangling surrogates: 0
All OpenClaw browser snapshot outputs are clean UTF-16: âœ…

(This output is from snapshotAria â€” the actual OpenClaw CDP code path
that internally calls findCursorInteractiveElements at cdp.ts:580
using the patched truncation at cdp.ts:623.)
```

All text nodes are free of dangling surrogates. The same `.replace()` is
applied identically in `pw-tools-core.snapshot.ts:80` and
`routes/agent.snapshot.ts:81`.

Reproduce:
```bash
node --import tsx proof-browser-openclaw-path.mts
```

## Tests

`extensions/browser/src/browser/browser-text-truncation.test.ts` â€” 6 new tests
covering ASCII pass-through, long ASCII truncation, dangling-surrogate removal,
multiple emoji, code-unit cap preservation, and complete-pair pass-through.
target.author: solodmd
